claims.text: Distributing 3 across x + 1 gives 3x + 3, so 3(x + 1) = 15 is equivalent to 3x + 3 = 15.
claims.why_supported: LibreTexts explicitly states that the outside factor multiplies each term inside the parentheses, and OpenStax requires using the distributive property to remove parentheses before solving.

claims.text: From 3x + 3 = 15, subtract 3 from both sides to get 3x = 12, then divide by 3 to get x = 4.
claims.why_supported: The LibreTexts worked example removes parentheses, subtracts the constant term, and then divides by the coefficient. OpenStax independently states to move constants and use the multiplication or division property to make the coefficient 1.

sources.title: Mathematics LibreTexts - Multi-Step Linear Equations
sources.copied_text: Using this property we multiply the number in front of the parentheses by each term inside of the parentheses.
sources.why_this_source_counts: It directly supports the corrected distribution and the worked multi-step pattern.

sources.title: OpenStax Algebra 1 - Explaining Acceptable Moves to Solve an Equation
sources.copied_text: Use the Distributive Property to remove any parentheses.
sources.why_this_source_counts: It independently confirms the remove-parentheses step and the general solve-equation workflow.
